entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
Hello @patrick_winkler ,
Is the parameter "adaptable in production" relevant for SAP S/4HANA private cloud or it doesn't matter if it's set to true or false?

We are implementing business configuration app as an alternative for SM30 following clean core approach by SAP and we are not sure which settings controls the editing behaviour in the production and QA systems.
Please suggest us in this

entry.title: SAP CAP - How to Set Attribute Type as Integer When Mocking User in package.json?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently mocking a user in my package.json for local testing in a CAP project, like this:

 

"gabriel": {
  "password": "@Teste123",
  "roles": ["AMS"],
  "attr": {
    "ams": "1"
  }
}

In the restrict rule, I need to compare the ams attribute as an integer:

{
  grant: ['READ'],
  to   : ['FORNECEDOR', 'TRANSPORTADOR', 'AMS'],
  where: '( CriadoPor = $user or Transportadora = $user ) or $user.AMS = 1'
}

This works fine in the deployed environment, where xs-security.json defines the ams attribute like this:

 
"attributes": [
  {
    "name": "ams",
    "description": "ams",
    "valueType": "int"
  }
]

However, during local development, the mocked ams attribute is always treated as a string (e.g., "1"), and the comparison with the integer 1 in the where clause fails.

Is there a way to define the type of a mocked attribute as int in package.json, or any workaround to handle this locally?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Available Budget for a WBS element in SAP Odata

entry.title: Re: Sales Return on the basis of Tax Invoice in Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi Mr Sahil, Thanks for replying,

Actually my client deals with MSIL company and they are getting back from MSIL( MSIL return the material but they are issuing Tax invoice against the material return, Now how my client will do this return process into the system.For Ex:

Client send material to MSIL with Invoice, then due to the product damage MSIL will return the material back to the client and issue Tax invoice against it.

So firstly how my client will do Entry into the system? and secondly how my client will send this material to there supplier? Please advise

Thanks

Mani Sajnani

entry.title: Procurement of Third Party Licenses/Subcriptions

We have a business requirement wherein the client sells third party licenses and subscriptions.  Given that these are not physical stocks, I would like to request advice from the community on how the procurement process will be so that client can sell this subscription/license.

Thanks,

Camille

entry.title: Issue with History Log for Profit Segment Fields in Sales Order
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We have an issue with the history log of all the fields in the Profit segment of the Sales order.

For example, on client110:
SO 1500000036
Item SO > Sheet Account Assignment > Profit Segment
I made a change to the "Sales Office" field, updating it from BT0 to YL5. However, I am unable to find the history log under Environment > Change.

Could you please help me check? Is it possible to view the history log for these fields?

entry.title: Transformation logic for SCD Type 2 in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello SAP Datasphere experts,

I am fairly new to SAP Datasphere and trying to find out and implement slowly changing dimension type2 in data sphere for the cost center object with Zemployee as an attribute. So if the employee change his department and belongs to a different cost center then it should keep the history.

I created a source table Sourec_CostCenter and Target table Target ZDIM_CostCenter_SCD2

I have all the required fields such as Cost center employee, from and To date, Flag and Record ID but I dont know where should I write or configure and option for SCD type 2. I was told to do it in the transformation but I cant even find the place or transformation where I can configure a logic for SCD type 2.

Can anyone please help me with the screenshot where to write/configure SCDtype2 settings and where do i find this transformation interface.

Will appreciate your help.

 

Regards

entry.title: SAP SNOW INTEGARATION INBOUND IDOC using CREMAS Msg.typ require failure or success status to SNOW
entry.content:
Hi, there is a requirement in the ECC system where SAP is integrated with SNOW via BTP CPI-PI.  When an Inbound IDOC is received from SNOW to SAP.  Present SNOW gets <TransferId> and <DbId> as a response whether the IDOC status is success or failure. SNOW is expecting the IDOC status with message details from SAP.  

I think it is achievable using SYSTAT01 (SAPs standard status iDoc). Can anyone suggest the steps required from the ABAP side to send the status back when an IDOC received?

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @matthias_schmalz 

Yes, the app is deployed in the HTML5 repository and is not launched via Workzone.
However, since we are using the provider-consumer model, all the HTML5 apps must be deployed in the provider subaccount, while they are accessed and launched from the consumer subaccount.
Then, with this kinnof setup, can I still embed my reusable component into the main app?

entry.title: Custom MDK code for barcode reading logic is not working on Zebra scanner device in SSAM 2410
entry.content:
Hi All,
We have upgraded SSAM to 2410 and the enhancements we've made for the Inventory Clerk persona. Specifically, we've implemented functionality that reads concatenated barcodes formatted as (Plant/SLoc/Material) and updates the corresponding fields in GI, GR, and PI screens. The enhancement has been successfully tested and is working as expected on both iOS and Android devices.

However, our client has now decided to switch to using Android Zebra scanner devices instead of the previously mentioned iOS/Android devices. Unfortunately, we are encountering an issue where the concatenated barcode reading is not functioning properly on the Zebra scanner devices.

Could anyone assist us in resolving this issue? Your expertise and support would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help.

bill froelich   jitendra kansal

@ArunachalamPonnurangam

Thanks
Madhukar G

entry.title: Re: 2505 C4C Release Content
entry.content: SAP Sales and Service Cloud 2505 Release Briefing - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: While creating a sales order I am getting an error as...
entry.content:
Hi Daniel_AC,

Please note that according to our Rules of Engagement: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag  GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content." We have edited your answer and added the GenAI-statement as we detected AI-generated content in your post. Thank you for your contribution in the SAP Community!

Best regards, 

Kavya

entry.title: Mitigated risk assigned to a position does not reflect to the user level analysis
entry.content: The requirement is to whoever occupies the position (HR object) which has risk that has been mitigated should also reflect in User Level Analysis.

entry.title: R2/AM-ISP/Build-System Status is not NOCO but Success Message pop-up is appearing as completed Sav
entry.content:
When setting any Notification to complete the NOCO is not successful because of failures in the custom validations which are done in the SAP provided customer exit EXIT_SAPLIQS0_001 include ZXWOCU10.

Even though the NOCO is not set the standard class CL_EAMS_BO_NOTIF, method SAVE adds a success message without checking the Notification NOCO status.

Can you help?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Manage characterstics as custom fields in Fiori apps

@Lalith_Perera : This app is released for public cloud for versions 2408 & 2502.

please check your system cloud version.

Regards,

Ninad

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention IWS - SAP Business Data Cloud - Driving Innovation (Virtual, May 15, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual

May 15, 2025

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you to the SAP MaxAttention Innovation Workshop focusing on SAP Business Data Cloud – Driving Innovation Across Data Landscapes”, taking place virtually on May 15th, 2025. Please find the current agenda here.

SAP Business Data Cloud (SAP BDC) is at the core of SAP’s modern data strategy, designed to transform how businesses manage, connect, and extract value from data. This interactive session will explore how customers can unlock agility, enable AI, and drive innovation by modernizing their data landscapes.

We will showcase:

  • Customer challenges and how SAP BDC addresses fragmented and siloed data.
  • Real-world experience from Henkel as an early adopter.
  • How AI and insight apps are embedded into BDC to enhance data usage.
  • Transition paths from classic BW and BW/4HANA to SAP BDC, including hybrid scenarios.
  • Partner ecosystems, reusable data products, and BDC object store innovations.

SAP MaxAttention services that support and safeguard your transformation journey.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, paul.schropp@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Can the Inventory valuation for sales order stock be changed from "M" to "A" in Public Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Dears,

For sales order stock (E stock), normally the inventory valuation is separated with the normal inventory stock.  For example, using moving average price, the sales order stock price will be different with the normal inventory moving average price.

Our requirement, even using sales order stock, we still want to use the same moving average price with the normal inventory stock.

In SAP OP version, by transaction code OVZG, we can change the account assignment group from M to A, then the valuation will be changed to A as well. A means "Valuation without reference to sales document".

But in SAP S/4 Public Cloud Edition, we cannot find the configuration path for this one, is it allowed to be changed in Public Cloud version 2502? Or need to activate some special scope item?

Thanks for your help in advance.

entry.title: CDPOS, CDHDR, SM19, SM20 Logs Confirmation
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Share the following  logs for both application and database levels:

1.

Whether each of the above software have feature (Application & Database Level):
- When changes were made
- Who made those changes
- What data was changed
- Whether all changes are logged and not just the last changes
- Whether the audit trail is stored on server located in India
- Whether audit trail covers all type of transactions

(Change include creating new data, deleting, updating data)

We want =  Share the CDPOS , CDHDR ,SM19 and SM20 logs of 15 samples of the April month(1st to 15th) and next 15 samples of the current month(15th to 31st) for application and database level
Share the CDPOS , CDHDR ,SM19 and SM20 logs of 15 samples of the April month(1st to 15th) and next 15 samples of the current month(15th to 31st) for application and database level.

2. 

User IDs used for changes are assigned to each individual and that User IDs are not shared.

We want = Share the CDPOS , CDHDR ,SM19 and SM20  logs of 15 samples of the April month(1st to 15th) and next 15 samples of the current month(15th to 31st) for application and database level
Share the CDPOS , CDHDR ,SM19 and SM20  logs of 15 samples of the April month(1st to 15th) and next 15 samples of the current month(15th to 31st) for application and database level.

 

Regards,

Amir Sayyed.

entry.title: Re: TARGET and SOURCE are not replaced for qRFC, tRFC, ID...
entry.content:
Hi Mikhail,

The first two screenshots appear to be related to the Solution and Logical Component Group in Solution Manager. The third screenshot seems to pertain to the Interface Monitoring configuration. Unfortunately, there is no option in the current solution manager interface monitoring settings to perform a mass update for tRFC, qRFC, or IDOC monitoring types. Are the ERP and S/4HANA merged in the LMDB after the system migration? Or if they are treated as different systems in the solman? If they are treated as different systems, I recommend reconfiguring the Interface Monitoring (ICMON). 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Cancel Mat Doc Error - Deficit of MCHB-CLABS 24,956 L...
entry.content:
Hello @ShivamL 

I guess you are trying to cancel a receipt. Such a cancellation is effectively a stock issue. The message says that you don't have enough stock on: 

  • material: 4600021186
  • plant: 5065
  • storage location: U007
  • batch: 9203171001

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

Nitesh

entry.title: Re: Different Budgets - just one should be visible
entry.content: Thank you a lot!! 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to use nested $filter inside $expand while workin...
entry.content:
Using a function in the columns selector allows you to use additional functions deeper in the query. So you can plug a 'where()' clause anywhere on your expands. Below 'where' should only be applied to the 'b.promotion' expand. See also CAP docs.

 

const results = await remote.run(
  SELECT.from(BusinessUnitAssignments)
    .columns(b => {
      b.businessUnitID,
      b.promotion(p => {
        p.promotionID,
        p.promotionPriceDerivationRules(r => {
          r.promotionPriceDerivationRuleID
        })
      }).where(
        { statusCode: 'AC', and: { filterBuId: 'CZP1%7C2B' } }
      )
    })
    .where({
      businessUnitID: CustomerID,
    })
)

 

The 'query-by-example' syntax of this where clause can get a bit complex/confusing, so it's also possible to write as a templated string. You won't have auto-completion and type checking on that level anymore so there is no real difference apart from readability:

.where( `statusCode = 'AC' and filterBuId = 'CZP1%7C2B'` )

hi,

we did some customizing for depreciation area for investment measures by accident in OABC. But we cannot remove it by error AC528. setting it to warning in OBA5 doesn't help.

How can we remove the wrong customizing?

kr

Nico

entry.title: Re: How to turn off the 'Add Comments | Refactor' automat...
entry.content:
Hi Mihaly,

I am happy to update you that a new setting is available since yesterday in productive landscapes. Uncheck it to turn off  'Add Comments | Refactor' actions.

Regards,

Rima

entry.title: SAP Business One Delivery Note and Serialnumber History
entry.content:
Hello together,

i am working on an Integration for a Ticket System with SAP Business One. I already have some integrations working like syncing orders or invoices, which is all done via the B1 Service Layer Rest API.

I am stuck on a serialnumber search for delivery notes or invoices. It seems that there are not any options via the API so i tried fetching the data directly from the database.

What i am looking is something like the serial number transaction view. When you are in an delivery note you can go to serial transactions and see what and where that serialnumber was connected to.

I cannot replicate that via API or SQL.

This is the best i have done:

 

SELECT 
    OSRI.ItemCode,
	OSRI.ItemName,
    OSRI.IntrSerial,
    OSRI.SuppSerial,
    OSRI.CreateDate,
	OSRI.Direction,
    OSRI.BaseType,
    OSRI.BaseEntry,
    OSRI.BaseNum
FROM 
    OSRI
WHERE 
    OSRI.SuppSerial = 'MYSERIAL'
ORDER BY 
    OSRI.CreateDate

The least i would need is to find delivery notes on where a specifc serialnumber is linked to.

entry.title: Re: Which BAPI can be used to Tick the "Delivery Complete...
entry.content: Dear Raymond, Can you please send me the screenshot on how to provide the input details in the BAPI "BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_TT".

entry.title: Re: Linked Analysis not working
entry.content:
Hello @Torbjorn,

To be honest I don't remember if I managed to resolve that one but I know there are some limitations: https://me.sap.com/notes/3315721/E .

And sometimes it's just not working for no reason, then you access it again and it works. You should use this functionality: "Filter on data point selection". 

Also you can open a ticket to SAP, sometimes it helps because it can be a bug.

Good luck!

entry.title: Re: MTO and return order material value
entry.content:
Hi varadha,

when you are following the MTO, cost of product is determined by strategy based on your configuration. Based on strategy first preference is given to sales order specific cost estimate. If sales order specific cost estimate is not available then cost from the material master is taken into consideration. The return order should also have the cost estimate with status VO. Then the goods are taken at sales order value. Ask your SD consultant to check the copy controls on return order and ensure cost estimate exists on the returns order. The cost of returns should be typically same as original sales order unless there is change in raw material or activity cost changes.

For your second question, once the item is received as sales order stock, it is blocked stock for sales order. Move it unblocked stock and reassign the stock to other sales order.

 

Regards

Satish

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

Hi All,

Happy to be a part of this community !!!

entry.title: Re: Settlement document - multiple tax codes
entry.content:
Hi Gauthier,

thx for your comments. I think from all of your points only transfer manager could cause this behavior.

Do you have any sepcial transfer group in mind? I cant find anything in our config that manipulates the MWKS2 (WBRP)

And how would the logic actually be if we have two tax codes? Would the MWSK2 field be empty? I tried to forcfully clear the MWSK2 field with transfer manager by transfering a "fixed value" but when I put "0" it makes the tax code actually "0" and system gives a tax error.

Any additional infos you might have to share here?

Many thx!

BR Jan

entry.title: Execute same SFC on multiple resources of a WorkCentre in SAP DM
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Requirement: To execute same SFC on multiple resources of a WorkCentre.

The client scenario involves starting Order A for 100 Qty on Resource A. However, during execution, they intend to utilize both Resource A and Resource B to complete the full 100 Qty—without splitting the SFCs.

Observation:

  1. We started an SFC with 100 Qty at Resource A and were unable to perform partial confirmation at Resource B.
  2. When attempting a labor-on at Resource B, the time tracking still reflects activity under Resource A.

    Open Points / Clarifications Needed:

    1. Can we utilize both Resources A and B to execute single SFC without splitting?
    2. Can we perform partial or full confirmation (Yield & Scrap) from Resource B if the SFC was started at Resource A?
    3. Is it possible to log labor-on at Resource B if the SFC was initiated at Resource A?
    4. In ERP, the standard rate is defined per resource and per Work Center. Since both Resource A and B are being utilized in a single Work Center, how can we define the standard rate for both so we obtain accurate OEE data?
    5. Is there is any way to disable/ delete the existing default resource of workcenter or don’t show in POD selection to user?

      Regards,

      Utkarsh

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere license just to consume data in SAC
entry.content:
Yes, you need a DSP user to be able to access, as this is how security is managed.
If the user does NOT need to access the DSP environment and only needs to consume data, there is a CONSUMER role that does exactly that.

SAC and DSP must use the same identity provider and be configured with SAML2 to enable Single Sign-On. This way, once the user is authenticated in SAC, they can access DSP without having to re-enter their username and password.

From a financial perspective, there is no additional cost since user licenses in DSP are free, and as mentioned, DSP is billed based on CUs (memory, CPU, etc.).

Regards,

entry.title: Customer wants to allocate advance against the invoice
entry.content: While doing the invoice, is there any way to automatically adjust the advance with the invoice amount? Is there any way to do it?

entry.title: Re: Error While Marking Delivery Completion Indicator On PO

Seems the given error message is a custom message.

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @swati_suman2 

this is a different aspect. First your app and reuse component are deployed to the same space and running standalone on managed app router.
Next is that your app is multi tenant/SaaS enabled and subscribed to another sub account. 

How to enable an application for SaaS subscription is not described in this blog. Please have a look at https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/developing-multitenant-applications-in-cloud-foundry-environment?version=Cloud

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: Re: Can the Inventory valuation for sales order stock be...
entry.content:
Hello @Grace_Li 

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (2502), account assignment group configuration (e.g., OVZG) for valuation control between sales order stock (E-stock) and standard stock is not accessible, as IMG-level configuration is restricted in Public Cloud.

Solution
To apply the same moving average price (MAP) for both sales order stock and normal stock, you must:

  • Use valuation without reference to sales order (Account Assignment Group = 'A').
  • This setting is controlled by the "Item Category/Requirement Class" combination delivered via pre-configured scope items (e.g., BD9—Sell from Stock).

Let me know if you need further support

With regards

Chuma

Omar

entry.title: Re: Can the Inventory valuation for sales order stock be...
entry.content: @Chuma, Thank you very much for your solution, we'll test it and let you know the result. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: HANA Vector Engine and LangChain
entry.content: Right now, the plug-in support HANA Cloud standalone as a vector database. There are no plans yet - that I am aware of - to include Generative AI Hub.

entry.title: Re: DRC France FR_WHT_DAS2 - Occupation field showing no values
entry.content: Changed to localization tag.

entry.title: Re: DRC France FR_WHT_DAS2 - Occupation field showing no values
entry.content: Informed expert.

entry.title: Re: Revision level in inspection lot for EWM managed Stor...
entry.content:
SAP IM Stock Management does not take revision level into consideration.
The same happens with EWM.
QIE inspection lot is created via FM QPLEXT_INSPECTION_LOT_CREATE which then calls FM QPL1_INSPECTION_LOT_CREATE.

We have solved this issue by making use of implicit enhancement point at the start of FM QPL1_INSPECTION_LOT_CREATE.

First we check if lot origin is 17
Then we check the revision level in the relevant source document (eg: purchase order item EKPO)
The we take over the revision level in QALS-REVLV.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Payment Voucher and Payment Workflow
entry.content: @LucyLu

entry.title: The Next Generation of Business Leaders Must Master This…
entry.content:
The Next Generation of Business Leaders Must Master This…


Enterprise leaders today are navigating an era of rapid technological change. AI, automation, and cloud-based platforms are transforming industries, but many organizations are struggling to keep up. The leaders of the future must think differently, act boldly, and leverage technology strategically to drive business success.

What will define the next generation of enterprise leaders? Here are the key areas of mastery:

 1. Think Like a CEO, Act Like a Technologist
Leadership today is no longer about simply managing people—it’s about managing technology-driven transformation. The best leaders will:

✅Understand the power of AI and automation – Tools like SAP Joule bring AI-driven insights directly into business strategy.
✅Leverage real-time data – SAP Business Data Cloud enables leaders to make decisions based on live analytics rather than outdated reports.
✅Foster a culture of innovation – Driving technology adoption across business units ensures agility and resilience.

 2. Master AI, Automation, and Low-Code
Future leaders must not only understand business strategy but also how technology fuels it. The ability to:

🔹Deploy AI-driven automation through platforms like SAP Build Process Automation is critical.
🔹Optimize workflows with AI-powered analytics from SAP Business AI to drive efficiency.
🔹Enable citizen development through SAP Build so that business users can create their own digital solutions.

 3. Embrace Change as the New Constant
The business world is evolving faster than ever. Future leaders must:

🛠Be comfortable with continuous digital transformation.
📊Use data-driven insights to pivot strategies in real time.
⚙️Drive cross-functional collaboration between IT and business units to break down silos.

 The Future of Enterprise Leadership

To stay ahead, tomorrow’s leaders must:

✅Combine strategic vision with technological expertise.
✅Invest in AI, automation, and data-driven decision-making.
✅Create an adaptable, agile workforce ready for the digital future.

The leaders who embrace technology, data, and automation will be the ones driving the future of business.

What skills do you think the next generation of enterprise leaders need most?

entry.title: Navigating the SAP Business AI Landscape: Finding the Right Solution for Your Business
entry.content:
Summary

The SAP ecosystem provides a comprehensive suite of AI capabilities designed to empower organizations to drive efficiency and optimize productivity. A critical responsibility for decision-makers is determining which AI functionalities align with their business objectives, understanding their availability across on-premise environments, SAP Cloud solutions, and the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) as well as mapping these options to organizational needs.

Business Requirements

Your organization seeks to drive operational efficiency and boost employee productivity by leveraging SAP's Business AI capabilities. To make the most informed decision, you need a clear understanding of the available options within the SAP ecosystem. Key considerations include:

  • Should you activate Joule, SAP’s conversational AI copilot to empower your teams?
  • Are the embedded, pre-configured AI features sufficient or do they require customization to better align with your business goals?
  • Would a fully tailored AI solution, developed using SAP’s AI Foundation framework on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) better meet your unique business needs?

Solution Options

SAP offers multiple pathways to incorporate AI into your workflows, depending on your organizational needs:

  1. Joule:  SAP's AI copilot Joule simplifies tasks by leveraging natural language interactions, enabling process automation and delivering timely access to critical information. As agentic AI emerges within our integrated suite, it empowers customers to streamline and automate cross-functional business processes, fostering greater organizational alignment and breaking down operational silos.
  2. Embedded AI: Over seamlessly 200 pre-built and built-in AI features are integrated into SAP solutions, delivering immediate value.
  3. BTP AI Services: A collection of ready-to-use AI services, including data attribute recommendations, document extraction and personalized recommendations.
  4. Custom AI Development: Build fully customized AI solutions tailored to your unique business requirements by leveraging the full breadth of SAP BTP’s AI Foundation, including the Generative AI Hub and connect them to relevant Business Data & Context. 

SAP Business AI utilizes both SAP's proprietary technologies and leading partner solutions, including the latest Large Language Models, to provide advanced AI solutions across its portfolio.

Characterization of the Solution Options

Joule (Conversational AI Copilot):
Joule revolutionizes productivity by facilitating intuitive, conversational interactions. Rather than navigating through complex systems, users can simply articulate their tasks, and Joule seamlessly automates workflows or provides relevant information.  In alignment with our embedded generative AI use cases  across the portfolio, Joule’s AI agents are integrated across all areas of the business. 

Our fully integrated approach allows Joule Agents to work collaboratively across the platform, supporting every business function. This interoperability enhances their value, enabling them to address highly complex challenges and significantly boost productivity. Joule doesn't just improve isolated tasks, it redefines and optimizes entire business processes from end to end.

On top of Joule’s standard capabilities, you will soon also be able to build custom Joule skills and custom Joule Agents using Joule Studio, making it possible to even integrate data from external data sources into your workflows. And if you need help configuring an SAP System or want to boost your developer productivity, Joule for Consultants and Joule for Developers are there to support you.

Embedded AI Features:
SAP’s embedded AI capabilities are seamlessly integrated into its cloud solutions, delivering immediate value through pre-built features like Job Description Enhancement in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting or Automated Emission Factor Mapping in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management.

Additionally, SAP S/4HANA users can leverage Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) for end-to-end lifecycle management of machine learning models, supporting ABAP-based and side-by-side scenarios.

BTP AI Services:
SAP BTP offers a range of AI services, including:

  • Data Attribute Recommendation: Classify entities (e.g., products or users) and predict missing numerical attributes
  • Document Information Extraction: Automate the processing of business documents such as invoices and payment notes
  • Personalized Recommendation: Provide tailored recommendations using advanced machine learning techniques
  • SAP Translation Hub: Streamline translations for software and documents using AI-driven machine translation

Custom AI Development:
For unique business needs, SAP offers powerful tools to develop bespoke AI solutions.

SAP HANA on-premise: Supports machine learning use cases like classification, regression, time-series analysis and more.

SAP AI Foundation:  One-stop-shop for developers building customized AI application leveraging SAP BTP, including the BTP AI Services as well as:

  • Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core & AI Launchpad: Provides advanced tools for generative AI development, including access to over 30 embedding & large language models (LLMs), AI orchestration and prompt management and context grounding capabilities.
  • SAP AI Core & AI Launchpad: Manage AI assets with a scalable, hyperscaler-agnostic service that supports lifecycle management and integration with SAP solutions.
  • SAP HANA Cloud: Leverage the Vector & Knowledge Graph Engine to provide relevant context to LLM-based applications as well as advanced machine learning functions. 

Criteria to determine the most suitable target solution

To determine the most suitable AI solution for your organization, consider the following decision criteria:

  • Conversational Interface: Deploy Joule for chatbot-like interactions and streamlined workflows as well as agentic scenarios.
  • Rapid Deployment: Leverage Embedded AI or Intelligent Scenarios within SAP S/4HANA for minimal customization and immediate value realization. Utilize the AI repository's business value calculator for ROI assessment.
  • Workflow Integration: Implement BTP AI Services for seamless integration with existing processes (e.g. data classification, document processing).
  • Specialized Requirements: Develop custom AI solutions using SAP HANA or SAP BTP’s AI Foundation for highly specific business needs, leveraging advanced AI orchestration and integration capabilities.

Decision Tree

 Conclusion

The SAP ecosystem provides unmatched flexibility and capability to meet your AI-driven business goals. Whether you choose to enhance productivity with Joule, utilize pre-configured AI functionalities within SAP Cloud products or S/4HANA, or develop fully customized AI solutions using BTP and AI Foundation, SAP’s comprehensive suite of tools ensures that all business requirements can be addressed.

You can embark on your AI journey with SAP S/4HANA on-premise and progressively unlock the full spectrum of solutions with SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Private or Public Edition) and other SAP Cloud products. With SAP Business AI, your organization can:

  • Streamline operational efficiency and increase productivity.
  • Enhance decision-making with cutting-edge AI capabilities.
  • Develop tailored solutions to address even the most complex challenges.

By embracing SAP Business AI, organizations can unlock their full potential in the Intelligent Enterprise era.

entry.title: Smart change management with SAP Cloud ALM: Start small – grow with structure
entry.content:
Three proven approaches from practice

Three basic models have established themselves in many projects resp. in the maintenance, after the project phase.

1. Feature-driven
A lean approach in which the feature is the central control element. This is particularly suitable for agile teams with a high implementation speed and low formal approval requirements. The status flow of the feature enables transparent implementation without unnecessary complexity.

2. Requirement-driven
This approach adds the requirement element to the feature level. It serves as a control instrument for approvals and can bundle multiple features. Particularly suitable for organizations with moderate compliance requirements or increasing complexity.

3. Fully integrated
A comprehensive variant in which SAP Cloud ALM is integrated into an existing ITSM tool. This creates a continuous process chain from incident to change management to deployment in production. This option is particularly useful for companies with complex IT landscapes and high governance requirements.

An overview of these models is shown in the following figure.

 

Practical example: Fixed process flow (requirement-driven)

A practical example of the requirement-driven approach is the fixed process flow. This describes how changes – for example, corrections resulting from an incident – are controlled from the requirement to implementation in production.
 
The process comprises the following steps:
  1. Creation of a requirement in maintenance (or implementation project)
  2. Approval of the requirement
  3. Creation and development of the associated feature
  4. Transport and testing of the feature
  5. Approval for production and finally deployment

 

The possibility of automation, e.g., with SAP Build Process Automation, is also provided for in this process to reduce manual effort.
 
The process steps are summarized in the following diagram

 

There is no universal standard approach

Organizations should choose the entry point that suits their current requirements. The scalability of SAP Cloud ALM makes it possible to start with simple structures and add more complex control elements and integrations step by step as needed.

For companies that are currently considering optimizing or introducing change management, the models described offer proven approaches that can be flexibly adapted to different conditions.

entry.title: Re: how to configure a Business Partner (BP) to act as bo...
entry.content:
Hi @Srinivasarao-SAPSD ,

generally, it depends on what you have already configured for Business Partners in your system, because BP configuration is quite a broad topic. In terms on Customer/Vendor integration, probably the most important step is to link customer & vendor account groups to BP grouping in SPRO (assuming that account groups and BP groupings are already configured in your system):

SPRO -> IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Master Data Synchronization -> Synchronization Control -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Vendor Integration -> Field Assignment for Vendor Integration -> Assign Keys -> Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Vendor

SPRO -> IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Master Data Synchronization -> Synchronization Control -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Vendor Integration -> Field Assignment for Customer Integration -> Assign Keys -> Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Customer

Then you can try to create BP in appropriate group (used in the config above) and create it in roles for vendor and customer.

More details about BP configuration can be found in blogs, there is many of them in the community, for example:

SAP S4 HANA Business Partner (BP) end to end configurations 

Business Partner(BP) Complete Configuration Guide in SAP S4 HANA 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Linked Analysis not working
entry.content: Thanks! @rdum 🙂

entry.title: Re: Purchase order subcontracting BOM
entry.content:
Hi @alex_alex85 
If you want the the BOM components to be picked from Plant B > Change the BOM master record and Come to the PO in edit mode and click on 'Explode' option. 
If this is for one time activity, you may directly change the plant from A to B in PO itself.
the next postings like GR considers the components from PO only, not from BOM.
Please let us know If you need any further clarification.

entry.title: Re: Substitutions for Market segment using g/l account in...
entry.content:
Hello,

It seems to be available:

Rules for Substitution | SAP Help Portal

What's the error you get?

Do you mean you can't set G/L as condition?

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: 1902-Enable Self-Service Features in SuccessFactors Reporting

entry.title: Re: Purchase order subcontracting BOM
entry.content: Hi Vijay. Right. In this case we even do not need BOM, since I can manually enter components into Purchase order. The question is - how to bring other plant into Purchase order components automatically?

entry.title: Re: SAP PUBLIC CLOUD Recurring journal entry mass posting...
entry.content:
Hi,

The Manage Recurring Journal Entries app doesn't support a mass import. Are you talking about uploading journal entries massively?  Please refer to the SAP Help Portal for the app features: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/49d0f15801b640639e801c36e2936597.html

Alice

entry.title: Implementing Intermediary banks in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement to create intermediary bank chains for 30+ Business Partners and for each of these, the intermediary details will differ based on the currency of payment. Given this scenario, is there a solution to optimize/minimize the efforts of manually creating bank chain masters which can go beyond 100 at this point? Alternatively, do we have an excel upload functionality to create these?

Appreciate any leads on the inputs to setup this process in our system in an effective manner.

Regards,

Pooja S

entry.title: Re: Purchase requisition XXXX cannot be released
entry.content:
Hi @JiangXuan 
Hope for the required PR, Release strategy tab is appearing in the ME53N (either at header/Item level)
Please Identify which release group and release strategy appearing in the PR.
Go to the configuration of PR release strategy and simulate above 'release group' and 'release strategy'. The issue may exists here.
If the release strategy tab itself not appearing, then you may need to check with characteristic values are not matching with the required PR.
Please let us know If you find any Issue.

entry.title: Re: accounts receivable and advance accounts of the same sales order cannot be automatically liquida
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer as solution. You can un-accept it anytime if you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: In Public cloud getting error while updating the Juri...
entry.content: @LucyLu

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Table Reading in SAP ABAP & Pricing Integration
entry.content:
Hello,

Just a quick hint - next time, post such content as a blog post, and not a Q&A question 😉

Best regards

entry.title: Grow with SAP for Retail: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition retail, fashion, and vertical business
entry.content:
The evolution of SAP's retail systems

Having dedicated 30 years to SAP and the retail industry, I have had the privilege of witnessing the remarkable evolution of SAP's retail systems. From the first version specifically designed for retail in a ECC system to the continuous improvements over the years, culminating in the sophisticated cloud private edition for retailers, the journey has been extraordinary. And with this cloud private edition, we are still not at the end of this evolution! 

Begin of 2025, SAP proudly introduced the Retail Industry version of our multi-tenant SaaS ERP solution (general availability). This advanced system goes beyond the fundamentals of finance, procurement, and warehouse management to include specialized features such as store management, pricing, promotions, replenishment, allocations, and more. These processes are tailored to the retail sector, drawing on SAP's 50 years of experience collaborating with the world's leading retailers. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business empowers small and medium-sized enterprises to drive business growth and foster innovation. By standardizing retail-specific processes, this modern, agile, and scalable cloud ERP solution ensures the fastest time to value and the lowest total cost of ownership.

In simple terms, it’s a platform that allows retailers to focus on what they do best: delighting their customers. In an age where customer experience defines success, maintaining this focus is essential.

Are you ready to transform your retail business across the value chain?

Retailers are confronted with three significant challenges: consumers seeking personalized and instant experiences, competitors utilizing technology to drive innovation, and supply chains strained by global uncertainties. Within this crucible of challenges, I believe that Grow with SAP for Retail emerges as an essential lifeline for the contemporary retailer.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business is, at its heart, a holistic cloud ERP solution tailored to the demands of retail businesses. Its goal is to empower retailers to thrive in times of disruption. This solution facilitates the streamlining of business processes, effective data utilization, and flexible scaling.

How could it look like in practice? Let's explore some key aspects of this solution together

To provide a clearer picture, we will step into the shoes of different employees in a retail company and explore their unique challenges, needs, and how they gain advantages from SAP's Public Cloud. Moreover, I will share some example screenshots as a teaser to further illustrate this (the content was built by SAP and demonstrated on NRF 2025).

Melanie, the Procurement Specialist

Melanie, a Procurement Specialist, plays a key role in ensuring the profitability and customer satisfaction of her store by selecting the right products, negotiating prices, and keeping purchases within budget. She relies on sales data to identify products that are in demand.

However, her role comes with several challenges such as errors from manual data entry which lead to inaccuracies, disorganized vendor information increasing costs, limited visibility affecting inventory management, and difficulty in monitoring in-store customer demand for more accurate and timely decisions on stock replenishment and procurement.

To effectively manage these challenges, Melanie needs tools and strategies that allow her to monitor stock levels, assess product performance, handle delivery performance, manage supplier contracts, and analyze sales data for forecasting demand. Implementing solutions like setting automatic reorder points, optimizing purchase orders, and strategies to prevent stock-outs can maximize sales and enhance customer satisfaction.

Utilizing SAP Cloud Public Edition tailored for retail can significantly support Melanie in her role. SAP can help optimize inventory levels, reduce carrying costs through accurate sales forecasting, streamline invoice processing for better supplier compliance, and provide built-in tools for communication and approvals. This streamlines the entire purchasing process, making it more efficient and effective in meeting both the store's and customers' needs.

(The procurement overview dashboard of Procurement Specialist Melanie provides pre-built reports, easy access to precise data)

(The machine learning model for predicting delivery dates utilizes the company's data, incorporating factors such as transportation method, lead time, vendor history, and weather conditions)Phil, the Store Manager

Phil, as the Store Manager, holds the responsibility for the store's smooth operation. He is dedicated to achieving sales targets, maintaining customer satisfaction, and effectively managing his team. His role encompasses strategic planning, team leadership, and operational know-how to propel the store towards success.

However, Phil faces a set of challenges that include balancing inventory to align with customer demands while also minimizing carrying costs, contending with slow restocking processes which can result in empty shelves and lost sales, and dealing with operational inefficiencies that create bottlenecks, impair productivity, and cause delays. 

To address these challenges, Phil's strategy requires developing and implementing methods to meet sales goals and enhance profitability, managing inventory levels to prevent stock-outs and ensure product availability, getting a clear perspective on short-term and long-term promotional activities, and using customer data to personalize recommendations and promotions.

Utilizing the SAP Cloud Public Edition tailored for retail, Phil can gain a suite of benefits to streamline operations and boost efficiency. This includes integrated data facilitating quicker decision-making processes across sales, inventory, and customer operations; enhanced real-time collaboration with local suppliers, which helps in improving lead times and reducing restocking delays; and omnichannel integration that guarantees a consistent customer experience and synchronized inventory management across all platforms. This comprehensive approach not only aids in tackling existing challenges but also positions the store for lasting success.

(Phil opens his store overview page which gives him a snapshot of his daily store operations briefly)
(Demand Forecast Run shows the expected demand for the coming weeks including seasonality)Sarah, the Store Associate
 
Sarah is a dedicated Store Associate with a flair for customer service and teamwork. She helps customers find the right products and ensures smooth transactions through the point-of-sale system. Her duties extend beyond the counter, as she restocks shelves, crafts appealing displays, and double-checks that price tags reflect current offers. The harmony and satisfaction in her workplace motivate her to contribute actively to the store's success, aiming to provide every customer with a pleasant shopping journey.

Despite her enthusiasm, Sarah faces several hurdles in her day-to-day operations. Inventory management can be sluggish and imprecise, leading to situations where products are either unavailable or unnecessarily abundant. This misalignment can make product location cumbersome and detract from customer satisfaction. Additionally, inefficient checkout processes can cause delays, leaving customers feeling frustrated. Her current tools also limit her ability to access insightful data, making it harder to make informed decisions.

With SAP Cloud Public Edition tailored for retail, Sarah's workflow can be optimized, leading to greater productivity. Improved in-store pickup processes minimize revenue loss, ensuring customers receive their orders promptly. Real-time visibility into stock levels empowers her to update customers accurately, fostering trust and satisfaction. Optimized inventory management reduces unnecessary carrying costs, while smart restocking based on actual sales keeps shelves perfectly stocked.

By integrating smart technology, Sarah can overcome her hurdles and elevate both her efficiency and customer satisfaction. Better inventory tracking, streamlined transactions, and comprehensive reporting provide Sarah with the tools she needs to thrive in her role. With SAP, she gains more control over her processes, fulfilling her responsibilities with newfound ease and confidence.

(BOPIS enables store associates like Sarah to prepare online orders for fast, convenient in-store pickup)

Natalie, the Chief Financial Officer

CFO Natalie is responsible for monitoring the financial health of the company. Her primary role includes developing strategies to enhance profitability and sustain long-term growth while managing potential risks. She is also tasked with leading the financial reporting to provide precise insights into the company's performance.

To effectively fulfill her duties, Natalie requires tools that offer real-time financial insights for accurate reporting and decision-making. She also needs integrated financial and operational data to gain a holistic view of business performance, scalable financial management to support operations across multiple locations seamlessly, and automated tools for compliance and tax reporting to minimize manual labor.

However, she faces challenges such as delayed strategic decisions caused by inadequate financial visibility, limited scalability which results in bottlenecks, time-consuming and error-prone consolidation processes, and a lack of real-time performance insights.

By utilizing SAP Cloud Public Edition tailored for retail, Natalie can benefit significantly. SAP provides agile, real-time management with personalized dashboards, reports, and key performance indicators, enabling faster financial closings and smoother group reporting. Moreover, predictive analytics in SAP help with better cash flow management, budgeting, and profitability forecasting. Additionally, SAP's flexible, cloud-based financial management solutions scale with business growth and support omnichannel operations, overcoming previous limitations and driving efficiency in financial operations.

(Example of a unified view of financial performance by product and merchandise category)
Takeaways

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business offers significant benefits for retailers of small and medium sizes. This user-friendly software delivers a clear return on investment by providing ready-to-use industry best practices. It enables cost-effective implementations and minimal capital investments, supporting retailers' growth objectives. The state-of-the-art Software-as-a-Service model ensures a seamless cloud experience with the convenience of a fully managed solution and easy onboarding, allowing retailers to focus on their core business activities and customer satisfaction.

Further Reading

A great solution experience is SAP's innovative S.Mart Store in Walldorf! This cutting-edge shopping destination is redefining retail with the powerful combination of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business solutions, along with additional SAP and partner technologies. Check out this blog to learn more about it: S.Mart Store in Walldorf, it delivers an innovating shopping experience

You are SAP Partner and interested in more information?

  • ‼️SAP partners can discover valuable information on this dedicated SAP Partner Page: Explore SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, retail, fashion, and vertical business.

  • ‼️The four personas I presented to you are integral to this Interactive Value Journey. Please enter to view the complete E2E offline demo.

entry.title: Re: Can't copy existing app
entry.content: Same problem here - says "Failed to copy the app!" - any progress here? It's quiet from the SAP side, is this Build Apps no longer maintained at all?

entry.title: How to Integrate AI Models into SAP BTP with Python and TensorFlow
entry.content:
Prerequisites

Before we begin, make sure you have:

  • An SAP BTP account with access to:
    • SAP AI Core
    • SAP AI Launchpad
  • Python 3.8+
  • TensorFlow 2.x
  • Docker (for containerizing the model)
  • Basic knowledge of machine learning model development

Step 1: Train and Save Your TensorFlow Model

Let’s use a simple classification model trained on the Iris dataset.

python
CopyEdit
import tensorflow as tf
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense

# Load and split the data
data = load_iris()
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(data.data, data.target, test_size=0.2)

# Build the model
model = Sequential([
    Dense(10, activation='relu', input_shape=(4,)),
    Dense(3, activation='softmax')
])

model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='sparse_categorical_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
model.fit(X_train, y_train, epochs=10

# Save the model
model.save("iris_model")

Step 2: Create a Docker Image for Your Model

SAP AI Core requires your model to be served as a REST API. Use Flask and TensorFlow Serving or FastAPI.

Here’s a simple Flask app (app.py) to serve the model:

python
CopyEdit

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

model = tf.keras.models.load_model('iris_model')
app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/predict', methods=['POST'])
def predict():
    data = request.get_json()
    prediction = model.predict(np.array(data['inputs']))
    predicted_class = prediction.argmax(axis=1)
    return jsonify({'predictions': predicted_class.tolist()})

Now create a Dockerfile:
Dockerfile
CopyEdit

FROM python:3.8-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY . /app
RUN pip install flask tensorflow

EXPOSE 5000
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
Build and test your Docker image:
bash
CopyEdit
docker build -t iris-model .
docker run -p 5000:5000 iris-model

Step 3: Register and Deploy the Model to SAP AI Core

a) Push the Image to a Container Registry

Use DockerHub or any registry supported by SAP BTP:

bash
CopyEdit

docker tag iris-model your_dockerhub_user/iris-model
docker push your_dockerhub_user/iris-model

b) Define AI Core YAML Configuration

Create deployment.yaml and scenario.yaml files to define your AI scenario and runtime environment.

deployment.yaml
yaml
CopyEdit
apiVersion: ai.sap.com/v1alpha1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: iris-deployment
spec:
  image: your_dockerhub_user/iris-model
  replicas: 1
  protocol: REST
  port: 5000

scenario.yaml
yaml
CopyEdit
apiVersion: ai.sap.com/v1alpha1
kind: Scenario
metadata:
  name: iris-scenario
spec:
  deployments:
    - name: iris-deployment
      version: v1

Step 4: Deploy Using SAP AI Core CLI

Install the AI Core CLI and authenticate using your BTP credentials.

bash
CopyEdit
aic login
aic scenario apply -f scenario.yaml
aic deployment apply -f deployment.yaml

Once deployed, you’ll receive an endpoint URL to your model.

Step 5: Consume the Model in SAP Applications

Your model can now be consumed via REST calls in:

  • SAP Fiori apps (using JavaScript/Fetch)
  • SAP BTP extensions (Node.js or CAP framework)
  • SAP Integration Suite (using HTTP calls)

Example call using Python:

python
CopyEdit

import requests
response = requests.post(
    'https://your-aicore-endpoint/predict',
    json={'inputs': [[5.1, 3.5, 1.4, 0.2]]}
)

print(response.json())

Benefits of Using SAP BTP for AI Integration

  • Scalability: SAP AI Core handles deployment, autoscaling, and monitoring.
  • Security: BTP ensures enterprise-grade authentication and authorization.
  • Reusability: The same model can be consumed by multiple SAP modules.

Final Thoughts

Bringing your TensorFlow models into SAP BTP opens new doors for intelligent automation. With SAP AI Core and Launchpad, you can operationalize machine learning inside enterprise workflows without reinventing your infrastructure.

Whether you're an SAP ABAP developer learning Python, or a data scientist looking to integrate with enterprise tools, this tutorial bridges the gap.

entry.title: Fiori App Error : Could not instantiate data source "DS_1" for query "/ERP/SFIN_V01_Q2501".
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a problem when using app: Market Segments - Actuals(App Id- F0943A). It showed errors :

1. Could not instantiate data source "DS_1" for query "/ERP/SFIN_V01_Q2501".

2. Query /ERP/SFIN_V01_Q2501 is unknown.

How to fix these errors ?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: CREATED BY USER NAME IS NOT VISIBLE IN TEMPLATE FORM FIN_FO_CORR_INTDOC_ENTRY
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Would like to know how to make the name of the 'Created by User' be visible in the form with template FIN_FO_CORR_INTDOC_ENTRY. Currently, the display is the user ID only. 

Attached screenshots are the current and 'to be' format in the form.

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Thank You 

Regards,

Melle

entry.title: Re: Consuming complex Datasphere models
entry.content:
Hey @hleboeuf ,

The connection between PowerBI and Datasphere is managed by the PowerBI client / driver.

Unfortunately, PowerBI's OData feed only supports import models (See Microsoft blog here)
If you need the live connection, you can find the reference here for DirectQuery.

Interestingly, DirectQuery is supported for both SAP BW and SAP HANA. 
Datasphere does provide an HDI component and a HANA Cloud instance with your development space, so maybe there is a way.

I think I'll look into that one next 😄

entry.title: Re: Consuming complex Datasphere models
entry.content:
Hey @BenedictV ,

Thank you!

entry.title: UI5 autofocus
entry.content:
Problem:

I used UI5 to create my own website and aimed to make it look different from a typical UI5 application. During this process, I encountered an issue where UI5 would automatically scroll to certain sections when navigating between pages. After some investigation, I discovered the cause: a button positioned at the bottom of the page. UI5 automatically sets the focus on the first clickable element it finds, which in this case, caused the page to scroll down.

 

Although I removed the button, you can still check out my site at https://wouter.lemaire.tech .

 

I also discovered that many others have faced similar issues with UI5 automatically managing focus and causing unexpected scrolling behavior:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/stop-focus-on-control/qaq-p/11295709

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/prevent-autofocus-on-sap-ui-unified-calendar/qaq-p/11173411

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/disable-soft-keyboard-or-autofocus-in-sapui5-mobile/qaq-p/682939

 

Solution:

In my case, the solution turned out to be quite simple—I just needed to disable the autoFocus property of the App control. I wasn’t aware of this functionality, and despite searching through numerous posts about focus issues on the SAP Community, I didn’t come across this solution. Here’s how I solved it:

entry.title: Re: Understanding Fragments based Adobe form building using LiveCycle version 10+ with OData as back
entry.content:
Hi @robinthakral ,

First very thanks for your kind words!

I have never utilized the "Manage Logo" feature yet.

But if the Manage Logo can be set only for Master Forms then we don't seem to have a choice here.

By the way apart from "SE78 BMP image, string" option we can directly paste the Logo in the Form itself.

Thanks,

Sijin
